rulings.Case Title: LILY CHAN VS. JUN XIANG LIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer By Jin Fan He To The First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of P/A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 30, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT JIN FAN HE's Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint. Defendant He's demurrer is overruled as to all causes of action except the 11th cause of action for declaratory relief. The demurrer is sustained as to defendant He without leave to amend as to the 11th Cause of action, Declaratory relief, under CCP 1061. That cause of action is unnecessary because the Breach of Contract cause of action will resolve the issues identified in the declaratory relief cause of action. Defendant is granted 10 days to file and serve an answer. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM ROUSE VS. CHEVRON CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Strike Portions Of Pltf'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 30, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC.'s (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CHEVRON) Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's Complaint. Defendant's motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to the allegations regarding injury to "face, mouth area, scalp, hands, and respiratory system" and with leave to amend as to punitive damages and attorney's fees. Plaintiff is granted 10 day's leave to amend. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM ROUSE VS. CHEVRON CORP. et al

rulings.Case Title: USI HOLDINGS CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al VS. GORAN RYN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 30, 2009, line 9, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order. PLAINTIFFS APPLICATION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS CLAIM IS NOT BARRED BY THE DOCTRINE OF COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS NOT A FINAL DECISION ON THE MERITS. HUNTINGDON LIFE SCI., INC. V. STOP HUNTINGDON ANIMAL CRUELTY 129 CAL.APP.4TH 1228. PLAINTIFFS HAVE DEMONSTRATED THEY ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS OF THEIR CLAIMS BASED UPON DEFENDANT'S REPUDIATON OF THE NONSOLICITATION COVENANT IN THE STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT. ALLIANT INS. SERV. INC. V. GADDY 159 CAL.APP.4TH 1292. THERE IS NO INDICATION FROM THE TEXT OF THE EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT THAT IT IS INTENDED TO SUPERCEDE THE STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT. ALSO, SEE VACCO INDUS. V. VANDENBERG 5 CAL.APP.4TH 34. THE CONSIDERATION OF THE RELATIVE HARDSHIPS FAVORS GRANTING INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS NARROWLY TAILORED, ONLY PREVENTING DEFENDANT FROM DOING BUSINESS WITH 4 OF THE CLIENTS HE SERVICED WHEN HE SOLD HIS BUSINESS TO PLAINTIFFS. DEFENDANT OVER HIS LONG CAREER HAS BUILT UP NUMEROUS CONTACTS IN THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY AND WILL NOT SUFFER ANY APPRECIABLE HARDSHIP IF HE IS BARRED FROM SERVICING THOSE 4 CLIENTS FOR 3 YEARS. PLAINTIFFS ON THE OTHER HAND, WILL SUFFER SUBSTANTIAL INJURY AND HARM THROUGH LOSS OF BUSINESS AND GOODWILL. THE APPLICATION IS GRANTED. =(302/CWW/JW)
